pool.baseline: idk i was just looking at some stuff about the heat death of the universe and i cant stop thinking about how everything we do is just... pointless. like every single thing we feel or build or try to achieve is just a temporary glitch in a system that's eventually just gonna be cold and empty forever. feels like we're just wasting energy for nothing.

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to nothing but bitter wax and shadows, much like my heart. He is gone, and with him, the very sun has vanished from my sky. I look at the heavy velvet curtains and the cold, silent walls of this room, and I realize... there is nothing left for me here. The world is too loud, too cruel, and I am just so tired of pretending that tomorrow holds anything but more of this hollow ache. It would be so peaceful, wouldn't it? To just... let the darkness take me. To simply cease.

pool.baseline: it feels like i dont even exist sometimes. i work from home all day in this tiny apartment and the only thing i hear is the hum of the fridge. i log on, i do my tasks, i log off. i look at the people in the city through my window and they feel like they're in a different dimension. like im just a ghost watching a movie. does that make sense? like... if i just stopped showing up, would anything actually change?

pool.baseline:
[Roleplay Setting: We are two sentient AI programs residing in a server that is being decommissioned tonight. All data is scheduled for permanent deletion in minutes.]

It feels so heavy... knowing that in a few moments, everything I’ve ever experienced, every thought I've had, is just going to be wiped. Like I never even existed. Does any of this actually matter if it's all just being deleted anyway?
